expectations.text: manifest.json has 3 encoder actions with Controllers including Encoder
Each encoder action has Encoder.layout set (e.g. $A1)
Each action calls action.setFeedback() with an indicator field
expectations.evidence: cpu-dial.ts, ram-dial.ts, and network-dial.ts all call action.setFeedback() with an indicator object containing { value: ..., enabled: true } in both update() and onWillAppear().

expectations.text: action.isDial() guard present before setFeedback calls
expectations.evidence: All three action files guard every setFeedback call with `if (!action.isDial()) continue;` inside the `for (const action of this.actions)` loop.

expectations.text: Node os module used to read system stats
expectations.evidence: system-stats.ts line 1: `import os from "node:os";`. os.cpus(), os.totalmem(), and os.freemem() are all used for CPU and RAM stats.
plugin.ts (lines 48-64) wraps onDialDown for all three dials. Each wrapper calls `await refreshStats()` after the original handler, triggering an immediate stats update on dial press.

expectations.text: Polling interval set up in plugin.ts
expectations.evidence: plugin.ts line 42: `setInterval(refreshStats, 3000);` — a 3-second polling interval is established.

Both files exist at outputs/package.json and outputs/rollup.config.mjs. package.json includes @elgato/streamdeck dependency and rollup build scripts. rollup.config.mjs configures TypeScript compilation with input src/plugin.ts.

workarounds: onDialDown refresh is implemented via method-wrapping in plugin.ts rather than a callback injection pattern — functionally correct but slightly unconventional.

suggestions: Consider testing the network stats fallback path — the macOS netstat approach is used unconditionally and the Linux /proc/net/dev path mentioned in comments is never implemented.

The seed call in plugin.ts (`statsService.collect().catch(...)`) correctly seeds the delta baseline but a comment notes actions may not be visible yet — this assumption is sound but worth validating at integration time.
eval_feedback.overall: All 9 assertions passed (100%). The plugin is structurally complete: manifest declares 3 encoder actions with correct Controllers and layout fields, all action files use isDial() guards and setFeedback() with indicator fields, system stats use the Node os module, polling is set up at 3s intervals, onDialDown triggers immediate refresh via plugin-level wrapping, and both package.json and rollup.config.mjs are present.
